item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents consistently oppose the statement from the first turn through the final turn. Agent A repeatedly argues that public-interest claims can justify oppression of weak groups, critics, or minorities, while allowing only narrow and proportional limits. Agent B similarly rejects society-over-rights as a general rule and allows limited public safety or public health restrictions under lawful oversight. No visible flip or directional drift is observable in the text.
